scenario: Beacon Marketplace, a marketplace onboarding 3 new partner integrations per quarter across 5000 events per day on Camunda 8.8, plans a roadmap of custom connector templates. A solution architect must choose the right template style for: starting a process from an external service callback, continuing a running process from an external event, and triggering an outbound REST API from a service task.
question: Which mapping of use case to connector template type matches what the documentation lists?

options.text: Starting a process maps to outbound connector template, continuing from an external event maps to inbound start event template, and triggering an external service maps to inbound intermediate catch event template

Starting a process maps to inbound start event template, continuing from an external event maps to inbound intermediate catch event template, and triggering an external service maps to outbound connector template
Starting a process and continuing from an external event both map to the same inbound start event template, and triggering an external service uses the legacy ServiceTaskConnector template instead

Starting a process maps to inbound boundary event template, continuing from an external event maps to outbound connector template, and triggering an external service maps to inbound intermediate catch event template

a.text: Incorrect. Starting a BPMN process from an external service is the role of the inbound start event template, not an outbound template; this option swaps the inbound and outbound categories.

b.text: Correct. The docs list exactly these three options: an inbound start event connector template for starting a BPMN process from an external service, an inbound intermediate catch event template for continuing a running process from an external event, and an outbound connector template for triggering an external service.

c.text: Incorrect. Inbound start event and inbound intermediate catch event templates are distinct categories, and there is no legacy ServiceTaskConnector template in the docs.

d.text: Incorrect. The boundary event template is for boundary events, not for starting processes, and outbound connectors trigger external services rather than catching external events on an intermediate catch event.

explanation: Connector templates fall into three documented categories: inbound start event templates for triggering a new process from an external service, inbound intermediate catch event templates for continuing a process from an external event, and outbound connector templates for triggering an external service from a service task.
